sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes
Requested: Fund an 18-month transition of Blockfrost into a free, community-governed not-for-profit public API for Cardano, including operations during the transition period.
Recipient: Input Output Global / Blockfrost transition under Intersect-administered treasury management arrangements

Deliverables: Establish or host a not-for-profit entity and publish transition architecture, migration plan, and public usage dashboard in Q3 2026., Hold a board election via on-chain voting under public rules in Q4 2026., Move public API traffic for mainnet, preview, and preprod to the new stack and legally transfer Blockfrost IP in Q1 2027., Open a sustainability consultation and publish a long-term model proposal in Q2-Q3 2027., Operate the public API at minimum 99% monthly uptime with quarterly technical and budget reports during 2027.
Deadline/expiry: 18-month funding period with milestones from Q3 2026 through 2027

Claim (technical, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): Blockfrost is presented as a hosted REST interface that lets developers read from and interact with Cardano without running their own infrastructure or node.

Claim (adoption, independently_verifiable, high materiality): The proposal says Cardano Foundation developer ecosystem survey results show Blockfrost hosted-platform usage rose from 39.3% in 2022 to 71.5% in 2025.

Claim (adoption, proposer_asserted, high materiality): The proposal says Blockfrost served 781,000 unique visitors, more than 1.84 billion API requests, almost 700 requests per second, and over 7 TB of API data last month.
Claim (adoption, proposer_asserted, high materiality): The proposal says more than 50% of all Cardano transactions are submitted through Blockfrost in most epochs.
Claim (technical, proposer_asserted, medium materiality): The proposal says more than 100 Icebreakers have joined the decentralized Blockfrost operator effort.

Claim (governance, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal commits to transferring Blockfrost source code, trademarks, domain names, and associated assets to a governing not-for-profit entity as part of the transition.
missing: Independent evidence for: The proposal says Blockfrost served 781,000 unique visitors, more than 1.84 billion API requests, almost 700 requests per second, and over 7 TB of API data last month.
Independent evidence for: The proposal says more than 50% of all Cardano transactions are submitted through Blockfrost in most epochs.
Independent evidence for: The proposal says more than 100 Icebreakers have joined the decentralized Blockfrost operator effort.

Independent evidence for: The proposal states that long-term sustainability may come from paid tiers or a vendor-backed not-for-profit model, with future profits or fees supporting the public API and potentially returning proceeds to the Cardano Treasury.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Treasury analysis
findings: Six-month treasury flow regime: stressed (basis: total inflow (tau + donations) vs enacted withdrawals)
sections.conclusion: Treasury votes require a higher bar because they consume shared ADA and create precedent.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "Blockfrost is presented as a hosted REST interface that lets developers read from and interact with Cardano without running their own infrastructure or node." and clears the evidence gates.

Strongest NO: a material claim is unsupported — "The proposal says Blockfrost served 781,000 unique visitors, more than 1.84 billion API requests, almost 700 requests per second, and over 7 TB of API data last month." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit.
Strongest hold: if claims cannot be tied to replayable evidence, abstaining avoids overclaiming certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.
